titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to William J.H. Tyrrell 13, July, 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: William Upton Tyrrell expresses gratitude for a new penknife. A new draft arrived from "the strawberry jam eaters corps" several days before. Then the previous day strawberry jam came in the rations.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
13 July 1917 My dearest Daddy, The absolutely top hole parcel came
today and was most welcome. It
began with the knife is A1 and just
exactly what I wanted - not to heavy but
it contains all the necessary articles.
Thank you very much indeed for it.
Nothing could be better. All the
eatables also are beautiful. They came at
a very good time too as I am all
alone here for a couple of days. All
the others are out training and my
job is just to see that they rations
etc go out to them. You see cooks
and all are out so I am looking
after myself more or less so the parcel
is doubly acceptable. I expect we will very soon be leaving
here now. I was also delighted to get long + newsy letters from you all. Friday is always a great letter day
for some reason or other. I am at present lying out under
a nice big tree in the shade
and enjoying the fine weather. The book in the parcel also
arrived at an excellent time as
I finished one I had, last night and
was just hoping for another to
carry on with. We got a draft a few days ago
transferred fron the strawberry jam
eaters corps and yesterday up
came strawberry jam in the rations
so they must just have hit on the
ones who used to eat ours. It is
very good new jam No news. Will write again soon. With very best love to all from
Your most affec Son
Willie.
